title.zh: 卷二十八下 馮衍傳
title.raw: 卷二十八下 馮衍傳 Volume 28b: Biography of Feng Yan

sentences.zh: 建武末，上疏自陳曰：

translations.literal: At the end of the Jianwu era, he submitted a memorial stating his own case, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Toward the end of Jianwu, he submitted a memorial in his own defense:

sentences.zh: 臣伏念高祖之略而陳平之謀，毀之則疏，譽之則親。

translations.literal: Your minister humbly reflects that Gaozu's strategic grasp and Chen Ping's schemes—if one slanders them, one becomes estranged; if one praises them, one becomes intimate.
translations.idiomatic: I reflect on how Gaozu's strategic insight and Chen Ping's counsel worked: speak ill of them and you are pushed aside; speak well of them and you are drawn close.

sentences.zh: 以文帝以明而魏尚之忠，繩之以法則為罪，施之以德則為功。

translations.literal: By Emperor Wen's perspicacity and Wei Shang's loyalty—measured by law they become crime; applied with virtue they become merit.
translations.idiomatic: Consider Emperor Wen's clarity of judgment and Wei Shang's loyalty: judged strictly by the statutes they could be called crimes; weighed with humane virtue they count as service.

sentences.zh: 逮至晚世，董仲舒言道德，見妒於公孫弘，李廣奮節於匈奴，見排於衛青，此忠臣之常所為流涕也。

translations.literal: Coming down to a later age, Dong Zhongshu spoke of morality and was envied by Gongsun Hong; Li Guang exerted his integrity against the Xiongnu and was squeezed out by Wei Qing—this is what constantly makes loyal ministers weep.
translations.idiomatic: In more recent times Dong Zhongshu taught moral principle and was resented by Gongsun Hong; Li Guang held firm against the Xiongnu and was sidelined by Wei Qing—such things are why loyal ministers so often shed tears.

sentences.zh: 臣衍自惟微賤之臣，上無無知之薦，下無馮唐之說，乏董生之才，寡李廣之勢，而欲免讒口，濟怨嫌，豈不難哉！

translations.literal: Your minister Yan considers himself a mean and lowly official: above he has no Wei Wuji's recommendation; below he has no Feng Tang's advocacy; he lacks Mr. Dong's talent and Li Guang's standing—yet he wishes to escape slanderous mouths and remedy resentment and suspicion—is this not difficult!
translations.idiomatic: I am only a man of small station: I have no patron like Wei Wuji above me, no advocate like Feng Tang below, neither Dong Zhongshu's gifts nor Li Guang's stature—and still I hoped to escape backbiting and patch over ill will. How could that be easy?

sentences.zh: 臣衍之先祖，以忠貞之故，成私門之禍。

translations.literal: Your minister Yan's forebears, because of loyalty and steadfastness, brought disaster upon the private household.
translations.idiomatic: My ancestors were ruined in a family tragedy precisely because they stood loyal and true.

sentences.zh: 而臣衍復遭擾攘之時，值兵革之際，不敢回行求時之利，事君無傾邪之謀，將帥無虜掠之心。

translations.literal: And your minister Yan again encountered a time of turmoil and met the season of arms; he did not dare to turn his course to seek profit from the moment; in serving his lord he had no crooked schemes; as a commander he had no mind to plunder captives.
translations.idiomatic: I then lived through chaos and war; I did not twist my conduct to chase opportunistic gain; in serving the throne I harbored no deceit; leading troops I felt no urge to pillage.

sentences.zh: 衛尉陰興，敬慎周密，內自修敕，外遠嫌疑，故敢與交通。

translations.literal: Commandant of the Guards Yin Xing was reverent, cautious, and thorough; inwardly he disciplined himself, outwardly he kept clear of suspicion—therefore I dared to associate with him.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Xing, the Commandant of the Guards, was careful and discreet; he cultivated himself within and avoided every shadow of scandal abroad—so I deemed it safe to deal with him.

sentences.zh: 興知臣之貧，數欲本業之。

translations.literal: Xing knew your minister's poverty and repeatedly wished to establish him in a basic livelihood.
translations.idiomatic: He knew I was poor and more than once tried to set me up with land and a livelihood of my own.

sentences.zh: 臣自惟無三益之才，不敢處三損之地，固讓而不受之，昔在更始，太原執貨財之柄，居蒼卒之間，據位食祿二十餘年，而財產歲狹，居處日貧，家無布帛之積，出無輿馬之飾。

translations.literal: Your minister considered that he lacked the talent of the three benefits and did not dare to occupy the place of the three harms—firmly he declined and did not accept. Formerly under Gengshi, in Taiyuan he held the handle of goods and wealth; amid sudden crises he occupied office and drew salary for more than twenty years, yet his property grew narrower year by year and his dwelling daily poorer; his household had no store of cloth or silk, and abroad he had no adornment of carriage and horses.
translations.idiomatic: Knowing I was no fit companion for the three kinds of friend—and no fit associate for the three kinds that harm a gentleman—I steadfastly refused his offers. Under Gengshi I held fiscal office in Taiyuan; even in those frantic years I kept my post and my stipend for over two decades, yet my means shrank every year and my home grew poorer: no cloth piled in the house, no fine carriage when I went abroad.

sentences.zh: 於今遭清明之時，飭躬力行之秋，而怨仇叢興，譏議橫世。

translations.literal: Now encountering a clear and enlightened time, a season for correcting the person and exerting conduct, yet resentment and enmity arise in clusters, ridicule and criticism fill the age.
translations.idiomatic: Now, in an age meant for clarity and honest effort, I find myself hemmed in by grudges and smeared by gossip from every side.

sentences.zh: 蓋富貴易為善，貧賤難為工也。

translations.literal: For wealth and honor make it easy to do good; poverty and low status make it hard to achieve refinement.
translations.idiomatic: The rich and honored find virtue easy; the poor and obscure can scarcely win a fair hearing—no matter how carefully they live.

sentences.zh: 疏遠壟畝之臣，無望高闕之下，惶恐自陳，以救罪尤。

translations.literal: A minister distant and far from the furrowed fields has no hope beneath the high palace gates—trembling in fear he states his own case to redeem his faults.
translations.idiomatic: I am only a man from the fields, with no access to the high court; in fear and trembling I lay this before you, begging relief from my offenses.

sentences.zh: 書奏，猶以前過不用。

translations.literal: When the document was submitted, still because of former faults he was not employed.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial went up, but on account of his past errors he was still not taken back into service.

sentences.zh: 衍不得誌，退而作賊，又自論曰：

translations.literal: Yan could not realize his intent; he withdrew and composed a fu, and again discoursed on himself, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Frustrated in his aims, Feng Yan retired and wrote a rhapsody, appending this reflection:

sentences.zh: 馮子以為夫人之德，不碌碌如玉，落落如石。

translations.literal: Master Feng considered that in human virtue, one is not merely lustrous like jade or merely ordinary like stone.
translations.idiomatic: Master Feng held that true character is neither merely polished like jade nor merely plain like stone.

sentences.zh: 風興雲蒸，一龍一蛇，與道翺翔，與時變化，夫豈守一節哉？

translations.literal: Wind rises and clouds steam; now a dragon, now a snake—hovering with the Way, changing with the season—how could one cling to a single principle?
translations.idiomatic: Wind rises, clouds gather; one shifts between dragon grandeur and serpent concealment, soaring with the Way and bending with the times—why cling to one rigid pose?

sentences.zh: 用之則行，舍之則臧，進退無主，屈申無常。

translations.literal: Employed, then act; set aside, then hide; advancing and retreating without a fixed lord, bending and stretching without constancy.
translations.idiomatic: Called forth, I serve; dismissed, I withdraw; I follow no single patron, stiffening or yielding as circumstance demands.

sentences.zh: 故曰：『有法無法，因時為業，有度無度，與物趣舍。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: 'There is method and there is no method—take the season as your enterprise; there is measure and there is no measure—join with things in approaching or leaving.'
translations.idiomatic: The saying runs: 'Rules and freedom alike serve the moment; measure and abandon measure as things counsel advance or retreat.'

sentences.zh: 』常務道德之實，而不求當世之名，闊略杪小之禮，蕩佚人間之事。

translations.literal: 'One constantly pursues the substance of morality and does not seek fame in the present age; broadly neglects petty minor ritual; lets drift the affairs of the human realm.'
translations.idiomatic: Seek the marrow of the moral life, not the headline of the day; brush aside petty etiquette; do not be snagged by the world's small concerns.

sentences.zh: 正身直行，恬然肆誌。

translations.literal: Correct the body and walk straight; tranquilly give free rein to one's intent.
translations.idiomatic: Stand upright, walk a straight path, and let your purpose unfold in calm independence.

sentences.zh: 顧嘗好俶儻之策，時莫能聽用其謀，喟然長嘆，自傷不遭。

translations.literal: Only I once favored bold and generous stratagems; the age could not heed or employ my plans—sighing long, I wound myself for not having met the right time.
translations.idiomatic: I once pressed daring plans, but my age would not hear them; I can only sigh for a fate that never matched my hopes.

sentences.zh: 久棲遲於小官，不得舒其所懷。

translations.literal: Long lingering in petty offices, unable to stretch forth what he harbors in his breast.
translations.idiomatic: For years I stagnated in minor posts, never free to speak what was in my heart.

sentences.zh: 抑心折節，意淒情悲。

translations.literal: Suppressing the mind, bending the integrity—the intent is bleak, the feeling sad.
translations.idiomatic: I swallowed pride and bent my will; my thoughts turned bleak and my spirit grew heavy.

sentences.zh: 夫伐冰之家，不利雞豚之息；

translations.literal: Now the house that breaks ice [for the sacrifices] does not seek profit from chickens and pigs;
translations.idiomatic: Great houses commissioned to supply ritual ice do not haggle over pennies from chickens and pigs;

sentences.zh: 委積之臣，不操市井之利。

translations.literal: the minister who manages granaries does not grasp the profit of the marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: ministers who oversee the state's granaries do not peddle goods in the market for private gain.

sentences.zh: 況歷位食祿二十餘年，而財產益狹，居處益貧。

translations.literal: How much more one who has occupied posts and eaten salary for more than twenty years, yet whose property grows ever narrower and dwelling ever poorer.
translations.idiomatic: How much more I, who drew an official income for two decades yet grew poorer every year and narrower in my circumstances.

sentences.zh: 惟夫君子之仕，行其道也。

translations.literal: Only the gentleman's taking office is to practice his Way.
translations.idiomatic: The gentleman enters office only to carry his principles into practice.

sentences.zh: 慮時務者不能興其德，為身求者不能成其功，去而歸家，復羈旅於州郡，身愈據職，家彌窮困，卒離饑寒之災，有喪元子之禍。

translations.literal: He who weighs the affairs of the age cannot raise his virtue; he who seeks for himself cannot complete his merit. Leaving and returning home, again lodging as a traveler in provinces and commanderies—the more he holds office, the more his household is utterly poor and distressed; in the end he encounters the disaster of hunger and cold, and has the misfortune of losing his eldest son.
translations.idiomatic: Obsessed with current politics, I could not lift my character; scheming for myself, I never finished what I began. I quit and went home, then drifted again from province to province; the higher I climbed in rank, the deeper my family sank—until we faced cold and hunger and the bitter loss of my firstborn.

sentences.zh: 先將軍葬渭陵，哀帝之崩也，營之以為園。

translations.literal: The former general was buried at Weiling; when Emperor Ai died, it was laid out as a park.
translations.idiomatic: My late father, the general, lay buried at Weiling; when Emperor Ai died the tombs there were expanded into an imperial park.

sentences.zh: 於是以新豐之東，鴻門之上，壽安之中，地勢高敞，四通廣大，南望酈山，北屬涇渭，東瞰河華，龍門之陽，三晉之路，西顧酆鄗，周秦之丘，客觀之墟，通視千里，覽見舊都，遂定塋焉。

translations.literal: Therefore east of Xinfeng, above Hongmen, within Shou'an—the terrain high and open, extending broadly on four sides—south gazing at Mount Li, north linked to the Jing and Wei, east overlooking the Yellow River and Mount Hua, south of Longmen, on the road of the three Jins; west looking toward Feng and Hao, the mounds of Zhou and Qin, the ruins of Guanzhong—seeing through a thousand li and surveying the old capital—there he fixed the tomb.
translations.idiomatic: So I chose a site east of Xinfeng, above Hongmen, in the Shou'an district—high ground, wide prospects: Mount Li to the south, the Jing and Wei behind me to the north, the Yellow River and Mount Hua eastward, Longmen and the roads of Jin beyond; westward Feng and Hao, the old heights of Zhou and Qin, the wasted splendor of the heartland. From there one sees a thousand li and the old capital itself—that is where I laid the grave.

sentences.zh: 退而幽居。

translations.literal: He withdrew and dwelt in seclusion.
translations.idiomatic: Then I withdrew and lived in quiet retirement.

sentences.zh: 蓋忠臣過故墟而歔欷，孝子入舊室而哀嘆。

translations.literal: For the loyal minister passing an old ruin sighs with sobs; the filial son entering an old chamber laments and sighs.
translations.idiomatic: A loyal subject chokes up over ruined ground he once knew; a dutiful son cannot enter an ancestral hall without grief.

sentences.zh: 每念祖考，著盛德於前，垂鴻烈於後，遭時之禍，墳墓蕪穢，春秋蒸嘗，昭穆無列，年衰歲暮，悼無成功，將西田牧肥饒之野，殖生產，修孝道，營宗廟，廣祭祀。

translations.literal: Whenever he thought of his ancestors and fathers, who displayed abundant virtue before and hung vast merit behind—yet encountered the misfortune of the times, tombs overgrown and foul, spring and autumn offerings and seasonal sacrifices without proper ordering of senior and junior—old in years, late in life, grieving that he had no accomplishment—he intended westward to pasture and farm on fat and fertile wilds, increase livelihood, cultivate filial way, build ancestral temple, broaden sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: My forebears left shining virtue and a great name—yet the times broke them; their graves ran wild and our lineage offerings fell into disorder. Now I am old, with nothing achieved; I mean to go west to rich pasture, build up my household again, honor the dead with a proper shrine and fuller sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 然後闔門講習道德，觀覽乎孔老之論，庶幾乎松、喬之福，上隴阪，陟高岡，遊精宇宙，流目八纮。

translations.literal: Then closing the gate he lectured and practiced morality, perused the discourses of Kong and Lao, hoped perhaps for the blessing of [Prince] Song and Wangzi Qiao; ascended the slopes of Long, climbed high ridges, roamed his spirit through the cosmos, let his gaze flow over the eight cords.
translations.idiomatic: Then I would shut my gate to study moral teaching and read Confucius and Laozi, hoping for something of the immortals' ease; I would climb the Long ridges and high hills, send my spirit wandering through heaven and earth, and let my eyes sweep the eight reaches of the world.

sentences.zh: 歷觀九州山川之體，追覽上古得失之風，湣道陵遲，傷德分崩。

translations.literal: Passing through he observed the forms of the mountains and rivers of the nine provinces; tracing and viewing the winds of gain and loss in high antiquity—he pitied the Way's decline and mourned virtue's fragmentation.
translations.idiomatic: I would traverse the nine provinces, study how mountains and rivers lie, and read the ancient record of rise and fall—grieving that the Way had slackened and goodness broken apart.

sentences.zh: 夫睹其終必原其始，故存其人而詠其道。

translations.literal: Now one who sees its end must trace its beginning; therefore preserving the person one hymns his Way.
translations.idiomatic: To understand how things end you must find how they began; while these men live on in memory, I sing their Way.

sentences.zh: 疆理九野，經營五山，眇然有思陵雲之意。

translations.literal: Delimiting and ordering the nine wilds, managing and arranging the five mountains—dimly he had intent of thought to rise above the clouds.
translations.idiomatic: I ranged the nine domains and traced the five sacred peaks until my spirit lifted toward the clouds.

sentences.zh: 乃作賦自厲，命其篇曰《顯誌》。

translations.literal: Then he composed a fu to steel himself, naming the piece 'Manifesting My Intent.'
translations.idiomatic: I wrote a rhapsody to brace my own resolve and titled it 'Manifesting My Intent.'

sentences.zh: 顯誌者，言光明風化之情，昭章玄妙之思也。

translations.literal: 'Manifesting My Intent' means speaking of the disposition of bright enlightenment and transformation, and displaying the thought of the subtle and wondrous.
translations.idiomatic: The title refers to bringing one's moral purpose into clear light and giving honest voice to the deepest reflections.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: The words say:
translations.idiomatic: The text runs:

sentences.zh: 開歲發春兮，百卉含英。

translations.literal: The year opens and spring issues forth—ah, the hundred plants hold blossoms.
translations.idiomatic: The year turns; spring stirs; every stem bears budding flower.

sentences.zh: 甲子之朝兮，汨吾西征。

translations.literal: On the morning of the jiazi day—ah, swiftly I march west.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn on the day jiazi I set my course westward.

sentences.zh: 發軔新豐兮，裴回鎬京。

translations.literal: I set the carriage brake at Xinfeng—ah, I linger at Haojing.
translations.idiomatic: I lift the brake at Xinfeng and pace the ruins of old Haojing.

sentences.zh: 陵飛廉而太息兮，登平陽而懷傷。

translations.literal: I mount Feilian and heave a great sigh—ah, I climb Pingyang and harbor sorrow.
translations.idiomatic: I pass Feilian with a long sigh; climbing Pingyang, sorrow floods my breast.

sentences.zh: 悲時俗之險厄兮，哀好惡之無常。

translations.literal: I grieve at the age's customs perilous and straitened—ah, I mourn that likes and dislikes have no constancy.
translations.idiomatic: I lament a treacherous age and the whims that make praise turn overnight into blame.

sentences.zh: 棄衡石而意量兮，隨風波而飛揚。

translations.literal: Discarding the balance-stone and weighing by intent—ah, following wind and waves I soar.
translations.idiomatic: They cast away the fair scale and judge by whim; I am tossed on their shifting currents.

sentences.zh: 紛綸流於權利兮，親雷同而妒異；

translations.literal: Tangled and flowing toward power and profit—ah, intimate with thunder's echo and jealous of the different;
translations.idiomatic: The crowd chases power and profit, loves conformity, and hates every distinctive voice;

sentences.zh: 獨耿介而慕古兮，豈時人之所憙？

translations.literal: alone upright and admiring antiquity—ah, how could the men of the age delight in this?
translations.idiomatic: I alone stand apart and look to the ancients—how could this age approve?

sentences.zh: 沮先聖之成論兮，□名賢之高風；

translations.literal: Blocking the perfected discourse of the former sages—ah, [text missing] the noble wind of renowned worthies;
translations.idiomatic: They spurn the settled teaching of the sages and trample the lofty example of the worthy;

sentences.zh: 忽道德之珍麗兮，務富貴之樂耽。

translations.literal: Neglecting the precious splendor of morality—ah, they pursue the indulgent joy of wealth and honor.
translations.idiomatic: They neglect true moral beauty and chase the hollow delights of rank and riches.

sentences.zh: 遵大路而裴回兮，履孔德之窈冥；

translations.literal: Following the great road I linger—ah, I tread the subtle darkness of profound virtue;
translations.idiomatic: I keep to the great road and linger there, walking the deep, silent path of true virtue;

sentences.zh: 固眾夫之所眩兮，孰能觀於無形？

translations.literal: Surely this is what the crowd finds dazzling—ah, who can perceive the formless?
translations.idiomatic: the crowd gawks at glitter—who sees what has no shape?

sentences.zh: 行勁直以離尤兮，羌前人之所有；

translations.literal: Acting firm and straight thereby meeting blame—ah, indeed this is what the ancients also had;
translations.idiomatic: Frank conduct earns reproach—the ancients knew it too;

sentences.zh: 內自省而不慚兮，遂定誌而弗改。

translations.literal: inwardly examining myself I feel no shame—ah, thus I fix my intent and do not change.
translations.idiomatic: yet searching my heart I find no shame; my resolve is set and I will not bend.

sentences.zh: 欣吾黨之唐、虞兮，湣吾生之愁勤；

translations.literal: I rejoice in my kindred's Tang and Yu—ah, I pity my life's anxious toil;
translations.idiomatic: I exult in the days of Tang and Yu; I pity this anxious life I lead;

sentences.zh: 聊發憤而揚情兮，將以蕩夫憂心。

translations.literal: for a while venting indignation and raising feeling—ah, I thereby will wash away care in the heart.
translations.idiomatic: I voice indignation and let feeling rise, hoping to rinse away this grief.

sentences.zh: 往者不可攀援兮，來者不可與期；

translations.literal: What is gone cannot be grasped and climbed—ah, what is to come cannot be engaged by appointment;
translations.idiomatic: The past cannot be seized; the future will not be sworn;

sentences.zh: 病沒世之不稱兮，願橫逝而無由。

translations.literal: I worry at dying and the age not praising me—ah, I wish to flee sideways yet have no means.
translations.idiomatic: I dread leaving the world unsung; I would escape—but find no road.

sentences.zh: 陟雍畤而消搖兮，超略陽而不反。

translations.literal: I ascend Yongzhi and roam at ease—ah, I leap beyond Lüeyang and do not return.
translations.idiomatic: I climb the Yong altar and wander free; I pass Lüeyang and may never turn back.

sentences.zh: 念人生之不再兮，悲六親之日遠。

translations.literal: I think that human life does not come twice—ah, I grieve that the six kin daily grow distant.
translations.idiomatic: We live but once; my family slips farther from me day by day.

sentences.zh: 陟九□而臨戔嶭兮，聽涇渭之波聲。

translations.literal: I ascend the nine [peaks?] and overlook precipitous heights—ah, I listen to the wave-sound of Jing and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: I climb the Jiuzong heights and look down on the broken ridges; the Jing and Wei sing below me.

sentences.zh: 顧鴻門而歔欷兮，哀吾孤之早零，何天命之不純兮，信吾罪之所生；

translations.literal: Looking back at Hongmen I sob—ah, I mourn my orphan's early withering; how is Heaven's mandate not pure—ah, truly from my sins it arose;
translations.idiomatic: I turn toward Hongmen and weep for my child taken young; Heaven's will seems cruel—yet I know my own faults brought this;

sentences.zh: 傷誠善之無辜兮，賫此恨而入冥。

translations.literal: I wound that the truly good are guiltless—ah, bearing this resentment I enter darkness.
translations.idiomatic: the innocent good suffer still; I carry this bitterness into the dark.

sentences.zh: 嗟我思之不遠兮，豈則事之可悔？

translations.literal: Alas that my thought did not reach far—ah, could the deed then be regretted?
translations.idiomatic: Alas, I did not plan far enough—or can any past deed be recalled?

sentences.zh: 雖九死而不眠兮，恐余殃之有再。

translations.literal: Though nine deaths I do not sleep—ah, I fear my lingering misfortune may come again.
translations.idiomatic: Nine deaths would not close my eyes—I dread worse calamity still.

sentences.zh: 淚汍瀾而雨集兮，氣滂氵孛而雲披；

translations.literal: Tears surge and collect like rain—ah, my breath breaks in vast gusts like clouds parting;
translations.idiomatic: Tears stream like rain; great sobs tear from my chest like wind ripping clouds.

sentences.zh: 心怫郁而紆結兮，意沈抑而內悲。

translations.literal: The heart is pent and tangled in knots—ah, the intent is sunk and suppressed with inward grief.
translations.idiomatic: My heart knots tight; my spirit sinks under inward sorrow.

sentences.zh: 瞰太行之嵯峨兮，觀壺口之崢嶸；

translations.literal: I gaze down on Taihang's towering height—ah, I observe Hukou's jagged majesty;
translations.idiomatic: I look across Taihang's ridges and the fierce gorge at Hukou;

sentences.zh: 悼丘墓之蕪穢兮，恨昭穆之不榮。

translations.literal: I mourn the tombs' overgrown foulness—ah, I resent that senior and junior lack glory.
translations.idiomatic: I grieve for weed-choked graves and the broken order of our ancestral rites.

sentences.zh: 歲忽忽而日邁兮，壽冉冉其不與；

translations.literal: The years hurry and daily advance—ah, life slowly does not grant itself;
translations.idiomatic: The years race; my allotted span will not wait;

sentences.zh: 恥功業之無成兮，赴原野而窮處。

translations.literal: I shame at merit and enterprise without completion—ah, I go to the open wilds and dwell in uttermost retreat.
translations.idiomatic: I blush that I have achieved nothing; I flee to the wilds and hide.

sentences.zh: 昔伊尹之幹湯兮，七十說而乃信；

translations.literal: Formerly Yi Yin's seeking Tang—ah, seventy times he spoke and then was trusted;
translations.idiomatic: Yi Yin pled his case to Tang seventy times before he was believed;

sentences.zh: 臯陶釣於雷澤兮，賴虞舜而後親。

translations.literal: Gao Yao fished in Lei Marsh—ah, relying on Shun of Yu afterward he became intimate.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao angled in Lei Marsh until Shun raised him up.

sentences.zh: 無二士之遭遇兮，抱忠貞而莫達；

translations.literal: Lacking the two men's encounters—ah, embracing loyalty and steadfastness yet not penetrating;
translations.idiomatic: Without their timely meeting with power, I hold loyalty to no purpose;

sentences.zh: 率妻子而耕耘兮，委厥美而不伐。

translations.literal: Leading wife and children I plow and weed—ah, I entrust my excellence and do not boast.
translations.idiomatic: I lead my family at the plough and bury my gifts unsung.

sentences.zh: 韓盧抑而不縱兮，騏驥絆而不試；

translations.literal: Han Lu is checked and not unleashed—ah, the thoroughbred is hobbled and not tried;
translations.idiomatic: The keen hound is held leashed; the swift horse stands tethered, never raced;

sentences.zh: 獨慷慨而遠覽兮，非庸庸之所識。

translations.literal: Alone generous of spirit and gazing far—ah, not what the commonplace recognize.
translations.idiomatic: I alone rage and scan the distance—things the petty never grasp.

sentences.zh: 卑衛賜之阜貨兮，高顏回之所慕；

translations.literal: I deem low Zigong's piled goods—ah, I exalt what Yan Hui admired;
translations.idiomatic: I scorn Zigong's clever wealth and lift what Yan Hui loved;

sentences.zh: 重祖考之洪烈兮，故收功於此路。

translations.literal: I weigh heavy my ancestors' vast merit—ah, therefore I gather achievement on this road.
translations.idiomatic: My forebears' great deed weighs on me; on this path I seek to finish their work.

sentences.zh: 循四時之代謝兮，分五土之刑德；

translations.literal: Following the four seasons' succession—ah, I distinguish the five soils' virtue and severity;
translations.idiomatic: I follow the turning seasons and read each soil's blessing or bane;

sentences.zh: 林相麓之所產兮，嘗水泉之所殖。

translations.literal: Forests matching foothills' produce—ah, I taste what springs and streams nourish.
translations.idiomatic: I note what woods and foothills yield and sample what spring-water raises.

sentences.zh: 修神農之本業兮，采軒轅之奇策；

translations.literal: I cultivate Shennong's basic occupation—ah, I adopt Xuanyuan's strange stratagems;
translations.idiomatic: I return to Shennong's husbandry and borrow the Yellow Emperor's craft;

sentences.zh: 追周棄之遺教兮，軼范蠡之絕跡。

translations.literal: I pursue Zhouqi's remaining teaching—ah, I pass beyond Fan Li's vanished tracks.
translations.idiomatic: I follow the grain god's ancient lesson and leave Fan Li's track behind.

sentences.zh: 陟隴山以逾望兮，眇然覽於八荒；

translations.literal: I ascend Long Mountain to gaze beyond—ah, dimly I survey the eight wilds;
translations.idiomatic: I climb Longshan and strain my sight across the eight reaches;

sentences.zh: 風波飄其並興兮，情惆悵而增傷。

translations.literal: Wind and waves whirl and rise together—ah, feeling is melancholy and adds wound.
translations.idiomatic: Wind and wave surge together; my heart aches more.

sentences.zh: 覽河華之泱漭兮，望秦晉之故國。

translations.literal: I view the Yellow River and Mount Hua vast and boundless—ah, I gaze on Qin and Jin's old states.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow River and Mount Hua spread vast; I look toward the lost realms of Qin and Jin.

sentences.zh: 憤馮亭之不遂兮，慍去疾之遭惑。

translations.literal: I resent Feng Ting's not succeeding—ah, I am angry at Quji's encountering delusion.
translations.idiomatic: I rage that Feng Ting's plan miscarried and smart at Quji's fatal mistake.

sentences.zh: 流山嶽而周覽兮，徇碣石與洞庭；

translations.literal: Flowing mountains and peaks I scan all around—ah, I follow Jieshi and Dongting;
translations.idiomatic: I drift past sacred peaks, circle Jieshi, sweep toward Dongting;

sentences.zh: 浮江河而入海兮，溯淮濟而上征。

translations.literal: Floating the Yangtze and Yellow River I enter the sea—ah, I trace Huai and Ji upward in expedition.
translations.idiomatic: I ride the great rivers to the sea, then trace the Huai and Ji upstream.

sentences.zh: 瞻燕齊之舊居兮，歷宋楚之名都；

translations.literal: I look upon Yan and Qi's old dwellings—ah, I pass through Song and Chu's famous capitals;
translations.idiomatic: I pass Yan and Qi's old seats and Song and Chu's storied towns;

sentences.zh: 哀群後之不祀兮，痛列國之為墟。

translations.literal: I mourn the many lords' lack of sacrifice—ah, I grieve the feudal states become ruins.
translations.idiomatic: I mourn kings without heirs and kingdoms ground to dust.

sentences.zh: 馳中夏而升降兮，路紆軫而多艱；

translations.literal: Galloping the central Xia I rise and descend—ah, the road winds and turns with much hardship;
translations.idiomatic: I race the heartland's rises and falls on roads that twist and fight me;

sentences.zh: 講聖哲之通論兮，心愊憶而紛紜。

translations.literal: I expound the sage-wise men's penetrating discourse—ah, the heart is depressed and memories confused.
translations.idiomatic: I rehearse the sages' great themes until my heart knots with tangled thought.

sentences.zh: 惟天路之同軌兮，或帝王之異政；

translations.literal: Only Heaven's road shares one track—ah, or emperors and kings have differing government;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's law runs one track, yet kings rule each in his own way;

sentences.zh: 堯、舜煥其蕩蕩兮，禹承平而革命。

translations.literal: Yao and Shun blaze with their vast vastness—ah, Yu received peace and changed the mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Yao and Shun shone with effortless breadth; Yu took a settled age and forged a new mandate.

sentences.zh: 並日夜而幽思兮，終悇憛而洞疑；

translations.literal: Together day and night I darkly ponder—ah, in the end anxious and pierced with doubt;
translations.idiomatic: Day and night I brood until anxiety hollows me out;

sentences.zh: 高陽□其超遠兮，世孰可與論茲？

translations.literal: Gaoyang [text missing] his transcendently distant—ah, in the age who can discuss this with me?
translations.idiomatic: Gaoyang's line rose remote as heaven—who alive can weigh such things with me?

sentences.zh: 訊夏啟於甘澤兮，傷帝典之始傾；

translations.literal: I question Xia Qi at Gan marsh—ah, I wound at the imperial norm's beginning to tilt;
translations.idiomatic: I question Qi of Xia by Gan Pool and mourn where the golden age first cracked;

sentences.zh: 頌成、康之載德兮，詠《南風》之歌聲。

translations.literal: I praise Cheng and Kang's bearing virtue—ah, I chant the 'South Wind' song's sound.
translations.idiomatic: I hymn Cheng and Kang's virtue and the tune of the 'South Wind.'

sentences.zh: 思唐、虞之晏晏兮，揖稷、契與為朋；

translations.literal: I think of Yao and Shun's peace and ease—ah, I bow to Ji and Xie and make them friends;
translations.idiomatic: I dwell on the calm of Yao and Shun's age and would take Hou Ji and Xie as my companions;

sentences.zh: 苗裔紛其條暢兮，至湯、武而勃興。

translations.literal: Offspring and shoots profusely their branches spread—ah, down to Tang and Wu they surged to rise.
translations.idiomatic: Their line branched and flourished until Tang and Wu burst forth in power.

sentences.zh: 昔三後之純粹兮，每季世而窮禍；

translations.literal: Formerly the three rulers' purity—ah, each late age met utmost calamity;
translations.idiomatic: The three ancient kings were utterly good—yet each dynasty ended in ruin;

sentences.zh: 吊夏桀於南巢兮，哭殷紂於牧野。

translations.literal: I console Xia Jie at Southern Nest—ah, I weep for Yin Zhou at Muye.
translations.idiomatic: I mourn Jie of Xia besieged at Southern Nest and Zhou of Yin defeated at Muye.

sentences.zh: 詔伊尹於亳郊兮，享呂望於酆州；

translations.literal: I summon Yi Yin at Bo's outskirts—ah, I feast Lü Wang at Fengzhou;
translations.idiomatic: I call Yi Yin from the outskirts of Bo and feast Lü Wang by Fengzhou;

sentences.zh: 功與日月齊光兮，名與三王爭流。

translations.literal: Merit with sun and moon shares light—ah, name with the three kings vies in flowing.
translations.idiomatic: Their deeds shone with sun and moon; their fame runs alongside the three sage kings.

sentences.zh: 楊朱號乎衢路兮，墨子泣乎白絲；

translations.literal: Yang Zhu wails on the crossroads—ah, Mozi weeps over white silk;
translations.idiomatic: Yang Zhu cried out at the crossroads; Mozi wept for silk turned from white;

sentences.zh: 知漸染之易性兮，怨造作之弗思。

translations.literal: Knowing gradual dyeing changes nature—ah, I resent contrivance that does not think.
translations.idiomatic: They knew how habit alters the heart, and grieved that people plot without reflection.

sentences.zh: 美《關雎》之識微兮，湣王道之將崩；

translations.literal: I praise the 'Guan ju's' perceiving the subtle—ah, I pity the kingly way about to collapse;
translations.idiomatic: I honor how the 'Guan ju' read the small signs; I pity the royal way as it crumbled;

sentences.zh: 拔周唐之盛德兮，捃桓、文之譎功。

translations.literal: I pluck Zhou and Tang's abundant virtue—ah, I gather Huan and Wen's crafty achievements.
translations.idiomatic: I lift up the great virtue of the Zhou founders and collect the cunning triumphs of Huan and Wen.

sentences.zh: 忿戰國之遘禍兮，憎權臣之擅強；

translations.literal: I resent the Warring States' meeting calamity—ah, I hate powerful ministers' usurping strength;
translations.idiomatic: I rage at the disasters of the Warring States and hate ministers who seized strength for themselves;

sentences.zh: 黜楚子於南郢兮，執趙武於湨梁。

translations.literal: I dismiss the Chu lord at southern Ying—ah, I seize Zhao Wu at Ju-liang.
translations.idiomatic: I cast down the lord of Chu at his southern capital and hold Zhao Wu to account at Ju-liang.

sentences.zh: 善忠信之救時兮，惡詐謀之妄作；

translations.literal: I approve loyalty and good faith that rescue the age—ah, I hate deceitful plots' reckless making;
translations.idiomatic: I praise loyalty that saves the times and despise hollow stratagems;

sentences.zh: 聘申叔於陳蔡兮，禽荀息於虞虢。

translations.literal: I engage Shen Shu in Chen and Cai—ah, I capture Xun Xi at Yu and Guo.
translations.idiomatic: I would summon Shen Shu from Chen and Cai and seize Xun Xi where Yu and Guo fell;

sentences.zh: 誅犁鋤之介聖兮，討臧倉之訴知；

translations.literal: I punish Li's stubborn saintliness—ah, I chastise Zang Cang's accusing wisdom;
translations.idiomatic: I would punish poseurs who feign sainthood and bring down slanderers like Zang Cang;

sentences.zh: 巽子反於彭城兮，爵管仲於夷儀。

translations.literal: I humiliate Zifan at Pengcheng—ah, I ennoble Guan Zhong at Yiyi.
translations.idiomatic: I judge Zifan at Pengcheng and raise Guan Zhong at Yiyi with a noble title;

sentences.zh: 疾兵革之浸滋兮，苦攻伐之萌生；

translations.literal: I hate arms and leather's gradual spread—ah, I suffer attack and invasion's sprouting birth;
translations.idiomatic: I loathe the steady spread of war and the endless rise of campaigns;

sentences.zh: 沈孫武於五湖兮，斬白起於長平。

translations.literal: I sink Sun Wu in the five lakes—ah, I behead Bai Qi at Changping.
translations.idiomatic: I would drown Sun Wu in the five lakes and strike down Bai Qi at Changping.

sentences.zh: 惡叢巧之亂世兮，毒從橫之敗俗；

translations.literal: I hate clustered craftiness that disorders the age—ah, I poison vertical-horizontal alliance's ruining custom;
translations.idiomatic: I abhor the tricks that disorder the world and the alliance arts that poisoned custom;

sentences.zh: 流蘇秦於洹水兮，幽張儀於鬼谷。

translations.literal: I banish Su Qin to the Huan waters—ah, I imprison Zhang Yi in Ghost Valley.
translations.idiomatic: I would cast Su Qin into the Huan and shut Zhang Yi away in Ghost Valley.

sentences.zh: 澄德化之陵遲兮，烈刑罰之峭峻；

translations.literal: I clarify virtue-transforming's decline—ah, I blaze punishments' sheer harshness;
translations.idiomatic: I mourn how moral influence faded and how cruel punishments flamed;

sentences.zh: 燔商鞅之法術兮，燒韓非之說論。

translations.literal: I burn Shang Yang's methods—ah, I scorch Han Fei's discourse.
translations.idiomatic: I would burn Shang Yang's statutes and consume Han Fei's treatises in flame.

sentences.zh: 誚始皇之跋扈兮，投李斯於四裔；

translations.literal: I mock First Emperor's domineering—ah, I cast Li Si to the four wilds;
translations.idiomatic: I deride the First Emperor's tyranny and banish Li Si to the farthest frontier;

sentences.zh: 滅先王之法則兮，禍浸淫而弘大。

translations.literal: They extinguished former kings' norms—ah, disaster seeped and spread vast.
translations.idiomatic: They wiped out the laws of the ancient kings until disaster spread without end.

sentences.zh: 援前聖以制中兮，矯二主之驕奢；

translations.literal: Drawing former sages to establish the mean—ah, I correct the two lords' arrogance and luxury;
translations.idiomatic: I take the ancient sages as my measure and rebuke the pride and excess of two rulers;

sentences.zh: 馌女齊於絳臺兮，饗椒舉於章華。

translations.literal: I bring provisions to Nü Qi at Jiang Terrace—ah, I feast Jiao Ju at Zhanghua.
translations.idiomatic: I feed Nü Qi at the Jiang terrace and feast Jiao Ju at Zhanghua Pavilion;

sentences.zh: 摛道德之光耀兮，匡衰世之眇風；

translations.literal: I spread morality's brightness—ah, I rectify a declining age's feeble wind;
translations.idiomatic: I would unfold the light of the Way and set right the puny temper of a failing age;

sentences.zh: 褒宋襄於泓谷兮，表季劄於延陵。

translations.literal: I praise Duke Xiang of Song at Hong valley—ah, I display Jizha at Yanling.
translations.idiomatic: I honor Duke Xiang of Song at Hong and raise up Jizha of Yanling as a model;

sentences.zh: 摭仁智之英華兮，激亂國之末流；

translations.literal: I gather benevolence and wisdom's flower—ah, I stir chaotic states' dying current;
translations.idiomatic: I gather the flower of humanity and insight and try to stir the dying tide of ruined states;

sentences.zh: 觀鄭僑於溱洧兮，訪晏嬰於營丘。

translations.literal: I observe Zichan at Zhen and Wei—ah, I visit Yan Ying at Yingqiu.
translations.idiomatic: I watch Zichan by the Zhen and Wei and seek Yan Ying at Yingqiu.

sentences.zh: 日曀曀其將暮兮，獨於邑而煩惑；

translations.literal: The sun dimly is about to set—ah, alone I am vexed and troubled in the town;
translations.idiomatic: The sun sinks in haze; alone in my hamlet I fret and lose my way;

sentences.zh: 夫何九州之博大兮，迷不知路之南北；

translations.literal: How vast the nine provinces' breadth—ah, lost I know not road's south or north;
translations.idiomatic: How wide the nine regions—yet I cannot tell south from north;

sentences.zh: 駟素□而馳聘兮，乘翠雲而相佯；

translations.literal: Four plain [text missing] horses gallop—ah, I ride emerald clouds and wander;
translations.idiomatic: My team of white horses flies; I ride the green clouds and roam at ease;

sentences.zh: 就伯夷而折中兮，得務光而愈明。

translations.literal: I approach Boyi to find the mean—ah, I obtain Wuguang and grow clearer.
translations.idiomatic: I seek Boyi for balance and find Wuguang until my purpose shines clearer.

sentences.zh: 款子高於中野兮，遇伯成而定慮；

translations.literal: I knock for Zigao in the central wild—ah, I meet Bocheng and settle intent;
translations.idiomatic: I call on Zigao in the open fields and meet Bocheng until my mind steadies;

sentences.zh: 欽真人之德美兮，淹躊躇而弗去。

translations.literal: I admire the perfected man's virtue and beauty—ah, I linger hesitant and do not leave.
translations.idiomatic: I marvel at the worthies' virtue and linger, unable to tear myself away.

sentences.zh: 意斟愖而不淡兮，俟回風而容與；

translations.literal: Intent cautious and not bland—ah, I await the whirlwind and wander easy;
translations.idiomatic: My heart is anxious and never light; I wait on the turning wind and drift;

sentences.zh: 求善卷之所存兮，遇許由於負黍。

translations.literal: I seek Shan Juan's whereabouts—ah, I meet Xu You at Fushu.
translations.idiomatic: I search for Shan Juan's dwelling and meet Xu You at Fushu.

sentences.zh: 軔吾車於箕陽兮，秣吾馬於潁滸；

translations.literal: I set my carriage brake at Jiyang—ah, I feed my horses at Ying's shore;
translations.idiomatic: I halt my carriage at Jiyang and water my horses by the Ying;

sentences.zh: 聞至言而曉領兮，還吾反乎故宇。

translations.literal: Hearing utmost words I understand—ah, I return and turn back to my old house.
translations.idiomatic: Perfect teaching clears my mind; I turn back toward my home.

sentences.zh: 覽天地之幽奧兮，統萬物之維綱；

translations.literal: I view heaven and earth's hidden depth—ah, I comprehend the myriad things' guiding cords;
translations.idiomatic: I scan heaven and earth's hidden depths and grasp the threads that bind all things;

sentences.zh: 究陰陽之變化兮，昭五德之精光。

translations.literal: I investigate yin and yang's transformations—ah, I display the five virtues' essential light.
translations.idiomatic: I trace how yin and yang turn and show the bright essence of the five powers.

sentences.zh: 躍青龍於滄海兮，豢白虎於金山；

translations.literal: I leap the azure dragon on the grey sea—ah, I rear the white tiger on gold mountain;
translations.idiomatic: The azure dragon leaps from the deep sea; the white tiger is fed on Golden Mountain;

sentences.zh: 鑿巖石而為室兮，托高陽以養仙。

translations.literal: I cut rock to make a chamber—ah, I entrust myself to Gaoyang to nurture immortals.
translations.idiomatic: I hollow stone for a chamber and trust Gaoyang's line to school my soul toward transcendence.

sentences.zh: 神雀翔於鴻崖兮，玄武潛於嬰冥；

translations.literal: The divine sparrow soars at Hong cliff—ah, the Dark Warrior hides in deep gloom;
translations.idiomatic: The sacred bird wheels above Hong cliff; the Dark Warrior lurks in utter gloom;

sentences.zh: 伏朱樓而四望兮，采三秀之華英。

translations.literal: I crouch in vermilion tower and gaze four ways—ah, I gather three beauties' splendid blossoms.
translations.idiomatic: From a red tower I scan the world and pluck the splendid blooms of the immortals' herb.

sentences.zh: 篡前修之誇節兮，曜往昔之光勛；

translations.literal: I seize former cultivate men's boastful integrity—ah, I shine former times' gleaming merit;
translations.idiomatic: I claim the great integrity of the ancients and let their old glory shine again;

sentences.zh: 披綺季之麗服兮，揚屈原之靈芬。

translations.literal: I put on Qiji's lovely garments—ah, I raise Qu Yuan's spirit fragrance.
translations.idiomatic: I don the fair robes of Qiji and breathe Qu Yuan's haunting virtue.

sentences.zh: 高吾寇之岌岌兮，長吾佩之洋洋；

translations.literal: I raise high my crown's perilous peak—ah, I lengthen my pendants' flowing sway;
translations.idiomatic: My tall crown rises steeply; my long pendants sway with easy grace;

sentences.zh: 飲六醴之清液兮，食五芝之茂英。

translations.literal: I drink six sweet wines' clear liquid—ah, I eat five mushrooms' lush blossoms.
translations.idiomatic: I drink the six pure nectars and feed on the five magic fungi in bloom.

sentences.zh: 揵六枳而為籬兮，築蕙若而為室；

translations.literal: I weave six trifoliate oranges as fence—ah, I build huiru orchids as chamber;
translations.idiomatic: I fence my plot with six rows of bitter-orange and roof my cell with orchid and ruo;

sentences.zh: 播蘭芷於中廷兮，列杜衡於外術。

translations.literal: I sow orchid and iris in the inner court—ah, I array duheng along the outer path.
translations.idiomatic: I plant orchid and iris in the courtyard and line the outer walk with duheng;

sentences.zh: 攢射幹雜蘼蕪兮，構木蘭與新夷；

translations.literal: I cluster shegan mixed with miju—ah, I construct magnolia with xinyi;
translations.idiomatic: I mix shegan with miju and weave magnolia with fragrant bay;

sentences.zh: 光扈扈而煬燿兮，紛郁郁而暢美；

translations.literal: Light blazing and dazzling—ah, profusely lush and smoothly beautiful;
translations.idiomatic: Light blazes and dazzles; fragrance spreads rich and sweet;

sentences.zh: 華芳曄其發越兮，時恍忽而莫貴；

translations.literal: Flowers' fragrance brightly bursts forth—ah, the season flickers and nothing is prized;
translations.idiomatic: The blossoms flare with scent—yet in this drifting hour nothing seems worth cherishing;

sentences.zh: 非惜身之埳軻兮，憐眾美之憔悴。

translations.literal: It is not cherishing the body's hardship—ah, I pity the many beauties' withering.
translations.idiomatic: Not that I cling to ease for myself—I grieve that every lovely thing wilts.

sentences.zh: 遊精神於大宅兮，抗玄妙之常操；

translations.literal: I roam spirit in the great lodging—ah, I uphold dark subtlety's constant conduct;
translations.idiomatic: I let my spirit roam the vast inward hall and hold fast to the subtle constant Way;

sentences.zh: 處清靜以養誌兮，實吾心之所樂。

translations.literal: Dwelling in clarity and stillness to nurture intent—ah, truly my heart's delight.
translations.idiomatic: In quiet clarity I cultivate my purpose—this is what my heart truly loves.

sentences.zh: 山峨峨而造天兮，林冥冥而暢茂；

translations.literal: Mountains lofty reach heaven—ah, forests dark and deep flourish lush;
translations.idiomatic: Peaks rear toward heaven; the woods stand dark and thick;

sentences.zh: 鸞回翔索其群兮，鹿哀鳴而求其友。

translations.literal: The phoenix wheels seeking its flock—ah, the deer sadly cries seeking its mate.
translations.idiomatic: Phoenix wheels, calling its kind; deer cry out for companions.

sentences.zh: 誦古今以散思兮，覽聖賢以自鎮；

translations.literal: I chant past and present to scatter thought—ah, I view sages and worthies to steady myself;
translations.idiomatic: I read past and present to clear my mind and study the sages to steady my soul;

sentences.zh: 嘉孔丘之知命兮，大老聃之貴玄；

translations.literal: I praise Kong Qiu's knowing Heaven's mandate—ah, I magnify Old Dan's esteeming the dark;
translations.idiomatic: I honor Kong Qiu's acceptance of fate and Laozi's reverence for the hidden Way;

sentences.zh: 德與道其孰寶兮；

translations.literal: Virtue and the Way—which is the treasure—ah;
translations.idiomatic: Which shall we treasure—virtue or the Way?

sentences.zh: 名與身其孰親？

translations.literal: Fame and the person—which is intimate?
translations.idiomatic: Which is closer—reputation or one's own life?

sentences.zh: 陂山谷而閑處兮，守寂寞而存神。

translations.literal: I slope hills and valleys and dwell at leisure—ah, I guard silence and preserve spirit.
translations.idiomatic: I terrace hills and valleys and live withdrawn, guarding stillness and sustaining spirit.

sentences.zh: 夫莊周之釣魚兮，辭卿相之顯位；

translations.literal: Now Zhuang Zhou's fishing—ah, declining minister and chancellor's eminent posts;
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang Zhou angled by the river and refused the highest offices;

sentences.zh: 於陵子之灌園兮，似至人之仿佛。

translations.literal: Yulingzi's watering garden—ah, resembling the perfected man's semblance.
translations.idiomatic: Yulingzi watered his garden—almost the figure of a perfect sage.

sentences.zh: 蓋隱約而得道兮，羌窮悟而入術；

translations.literal: For hidden dimly one obtains the Way—ah, indeed in utmost enlightenment one enters the art;
translations.idiomatic: In modest retreat one finds the Way; at the limit of insight one masters the craft.

sentences.zh: 離塵垢之窈冥兮，配喬、松之妙節。

translations.literal: Leaving dust and filth's deep gloom—ah, I match Qiao and Song's subtle integrity.
translations.idiomatic: I leave the world's grime behind and aspire to the purity of Wangzi Qiao and Chisongzi.

sentences.zh: 惟吾誌之所庶兮，固與俗其不同；

translations.literal: Only my intent's hoped-for goal—ah, surely with custom it is not the same;
translations.idiomatic: What my heart hopes for has never matched the vulgar world;

sentences.zh: 既俶儻而高引兮，願觀其從容。

translations.literal: Already bold and loftily withdrawn—ah, I wish to observe its ease.
translations.idiomatic: Already I stand apart on high ground—I would watch how ease unfolds.

sentences.zh: 顯宗即位，又多短衍以文過其實，遂廢於家。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ming ascended the throne, again many belittled Yan for ornament exceeding substance—thereupon he was dismissed and stayed home.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ming took the throne, many maligned Feng Yan for rhetoric that outran the facts, and he was cast aside to live at home.

sentences.zh: 衍娶北地住氏女為妻，悍忌，不得畜媵妾，兒女常自操井臼，老竟逐之，遂埳壈於時。

translations.literal: Yan married a woman of the Zhu clan of Beidi as wife; she was fierce and jealous; he could not keep secondary wives or concubines; sons and daughters constantly themselves operated well and mortar; in old age finally he drove her away—thereupon he was obstructed and ill-starred in his time.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yan married a lady of the Zhu family of Beidi. She was violent and jealous, so he could keep no concubines; his children had to haul water and grind grain themselves. In the end, in old age, he turned her out. His fortunes were bitter thereafter.

sentences.zh: 然有大誌，不戚戚於賤貧。

translations.literal: Yet he had great intent and was not anxious over lowliness and poverty.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he nursed great ambitions and did not fret over poverty or low rank.

sentences.zh: 居常慷慨嘆曰：『衍少事名賢，經歷顯位，懷金垂紫，揭節奉使，不求茍得，常有陵雲之誌。

translations.literal: Dwelling constantly he sighed generously, saying: 'Yan in youth served renowned worthies, passed through eminent posts, embraced gold and hung purple, raised tally and bore missions—did not seek careless gain—constantly had intent to rise above the clouds.
translations.idiomatic: He often said with feeling: "In my youth I served famous men, rose to high office, wore the gold seal and purple ribbon, carried the imperial baton on missions, and never grasped at unworthy gain—I always aimed higher than the clouds.

sentences.zh: 三公之貴，千金之富，不得其願，不概於懷。

translations.literal: The three dukes' nobility, a thousand gold's wealth—if he did not obtain his wish, he did not harbor them in his breast.
translations.idiomatic: I never reached the honor of the Three Dukes nor wealth counted in gold—but because they were not what I truly sought, I do not resent their absence.

sentences.zh: 貧而不衰，賤而不恨，年雖疲曳，猶庶幾名賢之風。

translations.literal: Poor yet not declining, low yet not resentful—although years are weary and dragged, still perhaps near renowned worthies' wind.
translations.idiomatic: Poor but not broken, humble but not bitter—though age wears me down, I still hope to walk in the footsteps of the worthies.

sentences.zh: 修道德於幽冥之路，以終身名，為後世法。

translations.literal: I cultivate morality on dark hidden roads, thereby to complete life's name, as model for later generations.
translations.idiomatic: I polish character along hidden paths, that my name may endure and later ages may take me as their pattern.

sentences.zh: 』居貧年老，卒於家。

translations.literal: ' Dwelling poor and old in years, he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: " He lived poor and old and died at home.

sentences.zh: 所著賦、誄、銘、說、《問交》、《德誥》、《慎情》、書記說、自序、官錄說、策五十篇，肅宗甚重其文。

translations.literal: What he composed—fu, dirge, inscription, discourse, 'On Friendship,' 'Moral Injunction,' 'Cautious Feelings,' secretary's discourses, self-preface, official record discourse—fifty pieces in all—Emperor Suzong greatly esteemed his writings.
translations.idiomatic: He left fifty works—rhapsodies, elegies, inscriptions, essays, along with 'On Friendship,' 'Moral Injunction,' 'Cautious Feelings,' treatises on correspondence, a self-preface, notes on office, and policy papers—and Emperor Zhang thought highly of his prose.

sentences.zh: 子豹。

translations.literal: His son was Bao.
translations.idiomatic: His son was Feng Bao.

sentences.zh: 豹字仲文，年十二，母為父所出。

translations.literal: Bao's style was Zhongwen; at age twelve his mother was divorced by his father.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Bao, courtesy name Zhongwen, was twelve when his father divorced his mother.

sentences.zh: 後母惡之，嘗因豹夜寐，欲行毒害，豹逃走得免。

translations.literal: His stepmother hated him; once taking advantage of Bao's sleeping at night she wished to carry out poison harm—Bao fled and escaped.
translations.idiomatic: His stepmother hated him; once while he slept she tried to poison him, but he woke and fled to safety.

sentences.zh: 敬事愈謹，而母疾之益深，時人稱其孝。

translations.literal: Serving respectfully ever more careful—yet the mother hated him ever deeper; people of the time praised his filiality.
translations.idiomatic: He served her all the more carefully, yet she only hated him more; contemporaries praised his devotion.

sentences.zh: 長好儒學，以《詩》、《春秋》教麗山下。

translations.literal: Grown, he loved Ru learning; with the 'Odes' and 'Spring and Autumn' he taught below Mount Li.
translations.idiomatic: As a man he loved classical learning and taught the Odes and Spring and Autumn Annals below Mount Li.

sentences.zh: 鄉裏之語曰：『道德彬彬馮仲文。

translations.literal: The village saying went: 'Morality refined and ample—Feng Zhongwen.
translations.idiomatic: Local people said: "In virtue and learning none matches Feng Zhongwen."

sentences.zh: 』舉孝廉，拜尚書郎，忠勤不懈。

translations.literal: ' He was recommended Filial and Incorrupt, appointed Gentleman of the Masters of Writing, loyal and diligent without slack.
translations.idiomatic: " He was recommended as filial and honest, became a Gentleman of the Masters of Writing, and served with tireless loyalty.

sentences.zh: 每奏事未報，常俯伏省閤，或從昏至明。

translations.literal: Whenever memorial matters were not yet answered, he constantly prostrated himself in the secretariat chamber—sometimes from dusk to dawn.
translations.idiomatic: When his memorials went unanswered he would wait prostrate in the secretariat, sometimes from dusk until dawn.

sentences.zh: 肅宗聞而嘉之，使黃門持被覆豹，敕令勿驚，由是數加賞賜。

translations.literal: Emperor Suzong heard and praised him; he ordered a Yellow Gate attendant to bring a coverlet and place it over Bao, commanding not to startle him—thereby repeatedly adding rewards.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang heard of this and approved; he had a eunuch lay a blanket over Feng Bao without waking him, then rewarded him again and again.

sentences.zh: 是時，方平西域，以豹有才謀，拜為河西副校尉。

translations.literal: At this time they were pacifying the Western Regions; because Bao had talent and stratagem, he was appointed Assistant Colonel of the Western Regions in Hexi.
translations.idiomatic: The court was then pacifying the Western Regions; seeing Feng Bao's ability, they named him deputy colonel in Hexi.

sentences.zh: 和帝初，數言邊事，奏置戊己校尉，城郭諸國復率舊職。

translations.literal: At Emperor He's beginning, he repeatedly spoke on border affairs, memorializing to establish the Wuji Colonel; the walled states all returned to former duties.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor He he repeatedly advised on the frontier and memorialized for the Wuji colonelcy; the oasis kingdoms once again fulfilled their former obligations.

sentences.zh: 遷武威太守，視事二年，河西稱之，復征入為尚書。

translations.literal: He was transferred Governor of Wuwei; handling affairs two years, Hexi praised him; again summoned in as Master of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: He became governor of Wuwei; after two years the whole Hexi corridor praised him, and he was recalled to the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 永元十四年，卒於官。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year of Yongyuan, he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in Yongyuan 14.

sentences.zh: 論曰：夫貴者負勢而驕人，才士負能而遺行，其大略然也。

translations.literal: The judgment says: Now the noble rely on position and are arrogant to others; talented men rely on ability and abandon conduct—their general outline is thus.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: the privileged rely on rank and slight others; clever men trust their gifts and neglect conduct—that is the usual pattern.

sentences.zh: 二子不其然乎！

translations.literal: Did not the two sons conform to this!
translations.idiomatic: Were not these two men exactly such cases!

sentences.zh: 馮衍之引挑妻之譬，得矣。

translations.literal: Feng Yan's citing the analogy of picking a wife—he got it.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yan was right to compare choosing a wife to choosing allies.

sentences.zh: 夫納妻皆知取詈己者，而取士則不能。

translations.literal: Now in taking a wife all know to take one who scolds herself—but in taking gentlemen they cannot.
translations.idiomatic: Everyone knows to marry someone who will speak plainly—even harshly—about her own faults, yet no one applies that rule when recruiting officials.

sentences.zh: 何也？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why is that?

sentences.zh: 豈非反妒情易，而恕義情難。

translations.literal: Is it not that returning jealousy feeling is easy, while forgiving righteousness feeling is hard?
translations.idiomatic: Perhaps resentment comes easily to human nature, while generosity grounded in duty does not.

sentences.zh: 光武雖得之於鮑永，猶失之於馮衍。

translations.literal: Guangwu although obtained him in Bao Yong, still lost him in Feng Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Thus Guangwu gained Bao Yong but lost Feng Yan.

sentences.zh: 夫然，義直所以見屈於既往，守節故亦彌阻於來情。

translations.literal: Thus it is that righteous straightness is the reason for being humiliated in what is past, and preserving integrity therefore is also ever more blocked in what is to come in feeling.
translations.idiomatic: So it is: an unbending sense of right explains the slights of the past, and clinging to principle will only draw harder resistance from the world to come.

sentences.zh: 嗚呼！

translations.literal: Alas!
translations.idiomatic: Alas!

sentences.zh: 贊曰：譚非讖術，衍晚委質。

translations.literal: The eulogy says: Tan rejected prophetic techniques; Yan in his later years entrusted his person.
translations.idiomatic: The summation runs: Huan Tan spurned omen lore; Feng Yan, late in life, offered his allegiance to the throne.

sentences.zh: 道不相謀，詭時同失。

translations.literal: Their ways did not match in counsel; deceiving the times, together they failed.
translations.idiomatic: Their paths never met in purpose; each misread the age, and both were left in the cold.

sentences.zh: 體兼上才，榮微下秩。

translations.literal: Their persons combined the highest talent; glory was slight, rank was low.
translations.idiomatic: They had the endowment of the greatest ability, yet reaped scant honor and meager office.
